channel.title: NPR Topics: Technology
channel.description: Latest technology news and breakthroughs in technology, science, and industry. Download the NPR Technology podcast and Technology RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Melania Trump shares the spotlight with a robot at an education and technology event
item.description: The robot accompanied the first lady to the White House East Room for the final day of a summit she had convened with counterparts from around the world through her Fostering the Future Together global initiative.
item.content:encoded:
The robot accompanied the first lady to the White House East Room for the final day of a summit she had convened with counterparts from around the world through her Fostering the Future Together global initiative.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Jury finds Meta and Google negligent in social media harms trial
item.description: The jury ordered the companies to pay $6 million in damages over defective design. The landmark verdict may influence the outcome of 2,000 other pending lawsuits.
item.content:encoded:
The jury ordered the companies to pay $6 million in damages over defective design. The landmark verdict may influence the outcome of 2,000 other pending lawsuits.

(Image credit: Frederic J. Brown)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Your data is everywhere. The government is buying it without a warrant
item.description: Data brokers buy up huge amounts of information from cell phones and browsers to sell for targeted advertising. But the government, including ICE, also buys the data.
item.content:encoded:
Data brokers buy up huge amounts of information from cell phones and browsers to sell for targeted advertising. But the government, including ICE, also buys the data.

(Image credit: Mandel Ngan)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block

item.title: OpenAI pulls the plug on Sora, the viral AI video app that sparked deepfake concerns
item.description: OpenAI said Tuesday that it was "saying goodbye to the Sora app" and that it would share more soon about how to preserve what users already created on the app.
item.content:encoded:
OpenAI said Tuesday that it was "saying goodbye to the Sora app" and that it would share more soon about how to preserve what users already created on the app.

(Image credit: Michael Dwyer)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Judge says government's Anthropic ban looks like punishment
item.description: Tech company Anthropic, the maker of the Claude AI system, is suing the Trump administration over the government labeling it a "supply chain risk."
item.content:encoded:
Tech company Anthropic, the maker of the Claude AI system, is suing the Trump administration over the government labeling it a "supply chain risk."

(Image credit: Patrick Sison)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: New Mexico jury says Meta harms children's mental health and safety, violating state law
item.description: The jury agreed that Meta engaged in "unconscionable" trade practices that unfairly took advantage of the vulnerabilities of and inexperience of children. Jurors found there were thousands of violations, each counting separately toward a penalty of $375 million.
item.content:encoded:
The jury agreed that Meta engaged in "unconscionable" trade practices that unfairly took advantage of the vulnerabilities of and inexperience of children. Jurors found there were thousands of violations, each counting separately toward a penalty of $375 million.

(Image credit: Jim Weber/Santa Fe New Mexican)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump administration to pay French company $1B to drop U.S. offshore wind leases
item.description: TotalEnergies has agreed to what's essentially a refund of its leases for projects off the coasts of North Carolina and New York, and will invest the money in fossil fuel projects instead, the Department of Interior announced.
item.content:encoded:
TotalEnergies has agreed to what's essentially a refund of its leases for projects off the coasts of North Carolina and New York, and will invest the money in fossil fuel projects instead, the Department of Interior announced.

(Image credit: Thomas Padilla)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: 10 tried-and-true methods to stay off your phone, according to our readers
item.description: We asked our audience to share the creative ways they limit their own phone use. They range from the practical (keep your phone in another room) to the creative (pair your phone with a fun paperback).
item.content:encoded: We asked our audience to share the creative ways they limit their own phone use. They range from the practical (keep your phone in another room) to the creative (pair your phone with a fun paperback).
item.dc:creator: Mika Ellison

item.title: Billboard ads for tech startups in San Francisco are cryptic on purpose
item.description: The old-fashioned roadside billboard is hot property these days in San Francisco, with tech startups snapping up ad space. But it's often not clear what they're selling.
item.content:encoded: The old-fashioned roadside billboard is hot property these days in San Francisco, with tech startups snapping up ad space. But it's often not clear what they're selling.
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: It's called 'private credit' — and it could lead to big trouble on Wall Street
item.description: The risky lending business has been booming, but now its problems are becoming increasingly visible on Wall Street and beyond.
item.content:encoded:
The risky lending business has been booming, but now its problems are becoming increasingly visible on Wall Street and beyond.

(Image credit: Angela Weiss)
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan
